name: docassemble-demo
documentation:
See the [docassemble web site] for a description of **docassemble**
and installation instructions.

To get help with using **docassemble**, join the [docassemble Slack
group].

[docassemble web site]: https://docassemble.org
[docassemble Slack group]: https://docassemble.org/docs/support.html

modules.name: docassemble.demo.custombg
documentation: do not pre-load

functions.name: docassemble.demo.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.

functions.name: docassemble.demo.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

modules.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb
documentation: do not pre-load
Import any DAObject classes that you will need

classes.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.BankModel
documentation: SQLAlchemy table definition for a Bank

classes.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.CustomerModel
documentation: SQLAlchemy table definition for a Customer

classes.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.BankCustomerModel
documentation: SQLAlchemy table definition for keeping track of which Banks have which Customers

classes.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.db_get
documentation: The db_get function specifies how to get attributes from the DAObject for purposes of setting SQL column values

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.db_set
documentation: The db_set function specifies how to set attributes of the DAObject on the basis of non-null SQL column values

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.db_del
documentation: The db_del function specifies how to delete attributes of the DAObject when the SQL column value becomes null

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.has_customer
documentation: This is an example of a method that uses SQLAlchemy to return True or False

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.add_customer
documentation: This is an example of a method that uses SQLAlchemy to add a record to the BankCustomer SQL table
to indicate that a bank has a customer.  Note that it is designed to be idempotent; it will not add
a duplicate record.

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.get_customers
documentation: This is an example of a method that uses SQLAlchemy to return a list of Customer objects.
It uses the by_id() class method to return a Customer object for the given id.

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.Bank.del_customer
documentation: This is an example of a method that uses SQLAlchemy to delete a bank-customer relationship
Define Bank as both a DAObject and SQLObject

methods.name: docassemble.demo.reldemodb.BankCustomer.db_find_existing
documentation: A db_find_existing method is defined here because the default db_find_existing() method for
the SQLObject class tries to find existing records based on a unique identifier column indicated
by the _uid attribute.  Since the unique identifier for a bank-customer relationship record is
not a single column, but rather the combination of bank ID and customer ID, there is no _uid
column for the default db_find_existing() method to use.  But we can write our own method for
how to locate an existing record based on Python object attributes (.bank.id and .customer.id).

modules.name: docassemble.demo.accordion
documentation: do not pre-load

functions.name: docassemble.demo.accordion.start_accordion
documentation:
Returns HTML for the start of a series of accordion
sections. The end_accordion() function must be called at some
point later in the page, or else the HTML will be corrupted. If
you want the section to be open when the page loads, set
showing=True.

functions.name: docassemble.demo.accordion.next_accordion
documentation:
Returns HTML for ending a previous according section and
starting a new accordion section. Can only be used after
start_accordion(). If you want the section to be open when the
page loads, set showing=True.

functions.name: docassemble.demo.accordion.end_accordion
documentation:
Returns HTML for ending a series of accordion sections. Can
only be used after next_accordion or start_accordion().
